questions.q: V.K. Krishna Menon served as India's minister for which portfolio?
questions.explanation: Krishna Menon served as India's Defence Minister.

questions.q: Krishna Menon is especially remembered for a long speech at which international body?
questions.explanation: He delivered a marathon speech on Kashmir at the UN Security Council in 1957.

questions.q: Krishna Menon represented India at which world organisation?
questions.explanation: He served as India's representative at the United Nations.

questions.q: His 1957 marathon UN speech concerned which subject?
questions.explanation: His famous lengthy UN Security Council speech addressed the question of Kashmir.

questions.q: Krishna Menon is best described as a:
questions.explanation: He was a diplomat and statesman who held senior public offices.

questions.q: Krishna Menon was India's first High Commissioner to which country?
questions.explanation: He served as independent India's first High Commissioner to the United Kingdom.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was Krishna Menon born?
questions.explanation: Krishna Menon was born in the Malabar region of present-day Kerala.

questions.q: Krishna Menon's marathon UN speech is famous for its:
questions.explanation: The speech is remembered as one of the longest ever delivered at the UN Security Council.

questions.q: Krishna Menon was closely associated as a colleague of which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He was a close associate of Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.q: Before independence, Krishna Menon spent many years in which city working for the Indian cause?
questions.explanation: He spent decades in London, where he was active in promoting India's case for independence.

questions.q: In which year did Krishna Menon deliver his famous marathon speech on Kashmir at the UN Security Council?
questions.explanation: He delivered the marathon Kashmir speech at the UN Security Council in 1957.

questions.q: Krishna Menon became Defence Minister of India in which year?
questions.explanation: He was appointed Union Defence Minister in 1957.

questions.q: While in London, Krishna Menon was a founder and long-time secretary of which organisation promoting India's cause?
questions.explanation: He led the India League in London, campaigning for Indian independence.

questions.q: In which year was V.K. Krishna Menon born?
questions.explanation: Krishna Menon was born on 3 May 1896.

questions.q: Krishna Menon resigned from the post of Defence Minister in the aftermath of which event?
questions.explanation: He left the Defence Ministry following the 1962 India-China border conflict.

questions.q: Krishna Menon was associated with founding which well-known publishing imprint while in Britain?
questions.explanation: He is credited with helping found the Pelican Books imprint during his years in London.

questions.q: Krishna Menon led India's delegation to the United Nations during which decade most prominently?
questions.explanation: He prominently led and spoke for India's UN delegation during the 1950s.

questions.q: Krishna Menon was elected to the Lok Sabha primarily from a constituency in which city or region?
questions.explanation: He represented a Bombay (Mumbai) area constituency in the Lok Sabha during his ministerial years.

questions.q: Before becoming a cabinet minister, Krishna Menon held what diplomatic rank for India in the United Kingdom?
questions.explanation: He served as India's first High Commissioner to the United Kingdom from 1947.

questions.q: Krishna Menon was a member of which house of the Indian Parliament during part of his career?
questions.explanation: He served in both the Rajya Sabha and the Lok Sabha at different points in his career.

questions.q: In which year did V.K. Krishna Menon pass away?
questions.explanation: Krishna Menon died on 6 October 1974.

questions.q: Krishna Menon qualified professionally in Britain in which field?
questions.explanation: He trained and qualified as a barrister in Britain.

questions.q: Krishna Menon's marathon UN Security Council speech in 1957 is generally cited as lasting roughly how long in total?
questions.explanation: His Kashmir speech is generally reported to have run for roughly eight hours across sittings.

questions.q: Krishna Menon became India's first High Commissioner to the United Kingdom in which year?
questions.explanation: He was appointed India's first High Commissioner to the UK in 1947.

questions.q: In which English city had Krishna Menon earlier served as an elected local councillor before independence?
options: The London borough of St Pancras
questions.explanation: He served as an elected councillor in the London borough of St Pancras.

questions.q: Krishna Menon's term as Defence Minister ran approximately from 1957 to which year?
questions.explanation: He served as Defence Minister from 1957 until his resignation in 1962.

questions.q: Krishna Menon studied at which institution in London known for social sciences?
questions.explanation: He studied at the London School of Economics, among other London institutions.

questions.q: Krishna Menon contested his later Lok Sabha elections in the 1960s and 1970s mainly as a candidate associated with which orientation?
questions.explanation: In his later years he contested with support from left-leaning groups after parting from his earlier party affiliation.

questions.q: Krishna Menon's diplomatic work is closely linked to India's foreign policy of:
questions.explanation: He was a prominent articulator of India's non-aligned foreign policy at international forums.

questions.q: Krishna Menon was first sent to Britain in the 1920s in connection with which educational movement?
options: The national education movement led by Annie Besant
questions.explanation: He went to Britain in connection with the national education movement associated with Annie Besant's Theosophical circles.
